body:
## Summary
- add a main-branch canonical site deployment workflow using the Sepo preview API's canonical deploy mode
- publish the canonical URL as a GitHub Production deployment status using the workflow token's deployments permission
- default PR previews on for private repos unless AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY=off, relying on the Sepo private-site gate
- update docs for canonical deploys and the relaxed preview policy

## Notes
- The workflow intentionally does not update the repository sidebar homepage URL. That requires repository Administration: write permission, which GITHUB_TOKEN cannot grant; the Production deployment status provides the deploy URL without that permission.

## Validation
- actionlint .github/workflows/deploy-canonical-site.yml .github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml
- git diff --check

| coding_workflow/generic | Keep privileged workflows on trusted runtime code | pass | 8/8 | The canonical workflow runs on `push` to `main`, has narrow permissions, and production status creation is gated on a successful canonical deploy URL. [.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L18](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L18), [.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L97-L129](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L97-L129) |

[.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L97-L129](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L97-L129), [.agent/src/github.ts#L276-L303](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.agent/src/github.ts#L276-L303) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Put shared capabilities in runtime | partial | 4/7 | Preview deployment publishing already goes through `.agent/dist/cli/publish-preview-deployment.js`; the analogous canonical deployment status path is embedded directly in workflow shell. [.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L98-L109](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L98-L109), [.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L105-L129](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L105-L129) |

[.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L83-L92](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L83-L92), [.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L117-L129](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/4a5154d77a3ed62dda152ae9582f535ef8c4be6e/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L117-L129) |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric misses are concentrated in the new canonical deployment workflow: it duplicates reusable deployment/status behavior in shell and has no bounded transient retry around external calls.
- The fixed build-contract boundary and docs are otherwise aligned with the prior comments/preview work.
- Smallest useful follow-up: move canonical deployment status publishing into the `.agent` runtime helper/CLI surface and add focused bounded retry for transient OIDC, Sepo API, and GitHub API failures.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The canonical workflow embeds GitHub Deployment/status creation directly in YAML shell instead of extending the existing `.agent` deployment helpers, which weakens `reuse-existing-code` and `shared-capabilities-in-runtime`.
- **WARNING:** The new canonical deploy path performs external OIDC, Sepo API, and GitHub API calls with immediate failure behavior and no bounded retry/backoff for transient failures.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #36 adds a main-branch canonical site deployment workflow for Sepo-hosted sites. It builds the site, uploads the artifact, asks the Sepo preview API for a canonical deploy, and publishes the resulting URL as a GitHub Production deployment status. It also changes PR preview policy so private repos preview by default unless `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY=off`, with docs updated around the Sepo private-site gate. The PR intentionally avoids updating the repository sidebar URL because that would require broader Administration permissions than `GITHUB_TOKEN` provides.

## Review
The current PR state has addressed the earlier missing `AGENT_ENABLED` blocker. One remaining code-quality issue is still supported by the current diff and multiple review artifacts: the new canonical deployment status path duplicates existing `.agent` deployment helper patterns in workflow shell.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Canonical deployment status publishing bypasses existing `.agent` helpers | WARNING | The new workflow builds GitHub Deployment/status payloads and calls `gh api` inline, while the existing preview path routes this class of behavior through tested `.agent` helpers and CLI glue. |

## Progress
- The earlier reviewer finding that the new job ignored `AGENT_ENABLED=false` is fixed in the current head at `.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml` line 22.
- Current PR checks show the main `check` job passing, with preview deploy/teardown jobs skipped.
- Existing PR discussion already includes a top-level Rubrics Review warning about inline deployment/status publishing, so I did not post duplicate inline comments.
- No existing inline review comments or review threads were present, so nothing was replied to, minimized, or resolved.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Canonical deployment status publishing bypasses existing `.agent` helpers</summary>

**Cause:** The canonical workflow constructs deployment and status payloads directly in YAML shell at `.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml` lines 98-130. The preview workflow already uses `node .agent/dist/cli/publish-preview-deployment.js`, backed by shared helpers such as `createGitHubDeployment` and `createGitHubDeploymentStatus`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a small canonical deployment helper/CLI in `.agent` that reuses the existing GitHub Deployment/status helpers, covers the canonical payload shape with tests, and replace the inline `jq`/`gh api` block with a short CLI invocation.

**Comments:** Reviewers agreed this is a real reuse/runtime-boundary concern, but differed on severity: Claude called it blocking, while the Rubrics and Codex reviews classified it as a warning. Given the current fixed pause guard and passing checks, this synthesis treats it as a warning rather than a release blocker.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The remaining issue is a concrete, scoped branch change that can be handled safely by moving canonical deployment publishing into the existing `.agent` helper/CLI pattern with focused tests.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Move canonical GitHub Deployment/status publishing out of workflow shell into a tested `.agent` helper/CLI and update the workflow to call it.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

[.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L14](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L14), [.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L25-L98](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L25-L98) |

| coding_workflow/generic | Put shared capabilities in runtime | pass | 7/7 | Canonical GitHub Deployment/status publishing now lives in `.agent` runtime/CLI with tests, while workflows only invoke the CLI. [.agent/src/cli/publish-canonical-deployment.ts#L1-L37](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.agent/src/cli/publish-canonical-deployment.ts#L1-L37), [.agent/src/__tests__/preview-deployment-cli.test.ts#L169-L262](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.agent/src/__tests__/preview-deployment-cli.test.ts#L169-L262) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Bound transient retries | partial | 3/7 | GitHub Deployment/status calls now retry transient failures, but OIDC token fetch and Sepo deploy API calls in the shared deploy action still fail immediately. [.agent/src/github.ts#L111-L138](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.agent/src/github.ts#L111-L138), [.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L35-L44](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L35-L44) |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 4/7 | The deploy action fails on non-200 responses, but does not validate that a 200 response contains a non-empty, non-`null` `.url` before publishing `url` output. [.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L43-L46](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L43-L46) |

## Notes

- The current PR has fixed the prior runtime-boundary issue by moving canonical deployment status publishing into `.agent` helpers and adding retry coverage for GitHub API transient failures.
- Remaining rubric misses are narrower: manual dispatch of a privileged workflow is not constrained to trusted runtime code, and the shared Sepo deploy action still lacks bounded retries and strict URL-output validation.
- PR checks currently show the main `check` job passing; deploy and teardown preview jobs are skipped.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `workflow_dispatch` can run the privileged canonical deploy workflow at a selected ref while the job executes local checked-out actions/runtime with `id-token: write` and `deployments: write`. Constrain manual dispatch to the default branch or keep privileged runtime checkout pinned to trusted code.
- **WARNING:** The shared deploy action performs OIDC and Sepo API calls without bounded transient retry/backoff, so temporary 5xx/rate-limit/network failures still fail the deploy path immediately.
- **INFO:** Validate the Sepo deploy response URL before writing `url=$URL`; a 200 response with missing `.url` currently becomes a successful `null` output.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #36.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted human feedback in PR #36 that warranted a new or updated durable rubric. The substantive review points were agent-authored and already covered by active rubrics like `reuse-existing-code`, `shared-capabilities-in-runtime`, and `bounded-transient-retries`. The rubrics checkout remains clean.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #36 adds canonical deployment for `main` through the Sepo preview API, publishing the resulting URL as a GitHub `Production` deployment status without requiring repository Administration permissions. It also changes PR previews so private repositories preview by default unless `AGENT_PREVIEW_POLICY=off`, relying on the Sepo private-site gate. The PR factors shared preview/canonical deploy logic into a composite action and moves canonical GitHub Deployment publishing into `.agent` runtime helpers. The PR is already merged at head `26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7`.

## Review
The earlier runtime-boundary issue is fixed, and reviewers generally agreed the implementation is sound. The remaining concerns are hardening items around trusted privileged workflow dispatch and transient failure handling, not release blockers.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Shared deploy action has one-shot OIDC and Sepo API calls | WARNING | `.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml` fetches the OIDC token and posts to Sepo with immediate failure behavior, so transient 429/5xx/network failures can fail both preview and canonical deploys. |
| Canonical workflow manual dispatch can run privileged local runtime from selected refs | WARNING | The workflow has `workflow_dispatch`, `id-token: write`, and `deployments: write`, then executes local checked-out actions/runtime without constraining manual dispatch to trusted `main` code. |
| Deploy response URL is not validated before output | INFO | A 200 Sepo response with missing or `null` `.url` would still be written as the action output and step summary. |

## Progress
- The prior finding about inline GitHub Deployment/status publishing is fixed: canonical deployment publishing now lives in `.agent/src/github.ts` plus `publish-canonical-deployment`.
- Review artifacts report the main `check` job passing for head `26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7`.
- Existing inline comments and review threads are empty; no replies, minimizations, resolutions, or new inline comments were made.
- I skipped the suggested inline retry comment because current top-level PR discussion already clearly covers the same issue.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Shared deploy action has one-shot OIDC and Sepo API calls</summary>

**Cause:** The shared action runs direct `curl` calls for the OIDC token and Sepo deploy POST at [`action.yml#L35-L44`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L35-L44). The new `.agent` GitHub Deployment retry helper does not cover this earlier deploy path.

**Candidate solutions:** Add bounded retry/backoff around likely transient OIDC/Sepo failures, while keeping auth, validation, and malformed-response failures terminal. Alternatively, move OIDC+Sepo deploy into a `.agent` CLI so retry behavior can share runtime code.

**Comments:** Claude, Codex, and Rubrics all identified this as the main remaining reliability gap.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Canonical workflow manual dispatch can run privileged local runtime from selected refs</summary>

**Cause:** [`agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L11`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/workflows/agent-deploy-site-main.yml#L3-L11) enables manual dispatch with privileged permissions, and later runs local checkout/actions without a `main`-only dispatch guard.

**Candidate solutions:** Restrict manual runs with a job condition such as `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`, remove `workflow_dispatch`, or pin privileged runtime checkout/actions to trusted default-branch code.

**Comments:** This was raised by the rubrics review as a trusted-runtime concern; other reviewers treated the permission scope itself as narrow.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Deploy response URL is not validated before output</summary>

**Cause:** [`action.yml#L45-L46`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/26c78a2d0196120adf8c5c0b1d17f6f8cfe8a4a7/.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml#L45-L46) reads `.url` and writes it directly to `GITHUB_OUTPUT`.

**Candidate solutions:** Require a non-empty, non-`null` URL, ideally absolute `http(s)`, before writing the output or summary.

**Comments:** INFO-level cleanup; it can be folded into the retry/response-validation hardening if that file is touched.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
HUMAN_DECISION: PR #36 is already merged, and the remaining concerns are follow-up hardening/policy choices that should not trigger an automatic fix-pr pass without maintainer confirmation.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add bounded retry/backoff around the OIDC token fetch and Sepo deploy POST in `.github/actions/deploy-sepo-site/action.yml`, keeping auth and validation failures terminal.
- [ ] Restrict canonical workflow manual dispatch to trusted `main` runtime, or remove `workflow_dispatch` if manual canonical deploys are not needed.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Deploy canonical sites and allow private previews
